text: she moved the first night she danced here, in his dwelling. Just like the women at Kitty’s, with the vitality leeched from her face . “I knew how hard it was on Tabitha when I quit. I went out every day looking for scrap, but never got much. She never said anything, never complained, but… It was just on her face. She tried to hide it, but I knew. So, I went back to Kitty’s.” She laughed — a hollow sound, devoid of humor. “Just my fucking luck that he was there that night .” Ronin’s processors blazed through stored data, pulling cryptic things she’d said and fitting them into what she was revealing now. “Warlord,” he said, forcing himself to remain still. To listen to all of it . “He came and offered more credits than anyone ever had. For just an hour of my time, he said. I agreed. It would’ve fed me and Tabitha for a month, at least. So, I followed him out the back door. It was dark, and quiet, and I…I couldn’t. I couldn’t do it .” Lara clenched her jaw, face pale, brows furrowed. Her eyes shifted from side to side, as though she was watching the scene play out . “I felt…sick. Knowing that I was going to trade the only thing that still belonged to me, the only thing I had any say over, for some plastic chips…I just felt so sick. So ashamed, ‘cause Tabitha did it, but I just… couldn’t . I told him no , that I changed my mind. And he… hit me .” The statement hung in the air. Ronin had spent most of his time in the Dust, roving from one violent encounter to the next. He’d been attacked by bots and men, and had ended his attackers in most cases. It was survival, just as he’d once told her. But what Warlord had done…his survival hadn’t been in question. His actions weren’t justified, weren’t necessary … “I’ve been hit before, but never like that. I don’t know if I blacked out, or what, ‘cause when I opened my eyes, he was there above me, looking pissed. I gave you a chance to make this easy, but you’re just like the rest of your kind. Untrustworthy, weak, spineless sacks of meat. So, I’m going to fuck you in the dirt, where you belong . “He held me down,” she continued, eyes glistening, “and every time I screamed or whimpered or made any damn sound, he hurt me more. And there was so much fucking pain. He kept me there for exactly an hour, and when he was done, he threw the credits on the ground and walked away. There was blood everywhere, and I could barely move. I laid there a long time before I dragged myself home. And, as sick as it made me, I took the credits. The night you brought me here…that was the first time I’ve been in the Market since .” What words of comfort could he offer her, after such an experience? What words could even begin to set such a thing right? None. Not even actions — and already, he was running simulations of a direct attack on Warlord, determining if there were a way to end him forever before the gearheads had a chance to react — could undo what had been done. The anger her story sparked in Ronin couldn’t help her deal with what had happened . “I failed that night.” Her words hitched on a sob. “I failed my sister .” More tears spilled onto his arm, rolling to the bedding beneath them. Despite it all — despite having every reason to hate Ronin for what he was — she curled against him . “Everyone has limits,” he said, combing his fingers through her hair at the nape of her neck. “Everyone has lines they will not cross. You didn’t fail .” Ronin held her until she quieted, until her breathing slowed and evened out. Until she was asleep . As slowly and carefully as he could, he withdrew his arm from beneath her and eased back. The temperature on the surface of his skin plummeted. Lara stirred, making a soft, indistinct sound in her throat, and nuzzled her cheek into the bedding . Her tears had dried up, but the skin around her eyes was pink and irritated. Her lips were in a similar state, result of the kisses they’d shared . Even in sleep, she wasn’t still. Fascinated, he watched the gentle rise and fall of her chest as she breathed, the subtle shifting of her breasts. Their purpose was to provide nourishment to human young, but their skin was so smooth, and she’d reacted so strongly when he touched them — especially her nipples. He’d responded, too. Touching her and seeing, feeling, and hearing her reactions had heightened his own arousal beyond anything he’d previously experienced. Nothing was as it seemed on the surface when it came to humans . Or, at least, when it came to Lara Brooks . He pored over nearly two centuries of memories, over the faces of hundreds of other females. Ronin had encountered many attractive humans, with only one true commonality between them — they all bore some mark, left by the harshness of the world. Usually it was a dull gleam in their eyes. And there’d been so many synths; embodiments of physical perfection, possessing exact symmetry in their features and idealized proportions . He’d seen hair and eyes of almost the same shade as Lara’s. Skin as pale and creamy. He’d seen lithe legs and firm breasts, flared hips and delicate feet. Seen so many bodies that should have been more appealing, so many faces with fuller lips, or more defined cheekbones, or thicker lashes . What was it about this woman that drew him so completely ?
